ConstructorArguments: Entity Tags are strings you can set and check for on any entity. Internally these strings are tokenized and networked so they're also available clientside.

ConstructorArguments: Performs linear interpolation between this and the given transform.

Documentation.Summary: Performs linear interpolation between this and the given transform.

Params.frac: Fraction, where 0 would return this, 0.5 would return a point between this and given transform, and 1 would return the given transform.

Documentation.Summary: Disable the proxy temporarily

ConstructorArguments: Clear any interpolation and force us to reach our final destination immediately. If we own this object we'll tell other clients to clear interpolation too when they receive the next network update from us.

Documentation.Summary:
Clear any interpolation and force us to reach our final destination immediately. If we own this object
we'll tell other clients to clear interpolation too when they receive the next network update from us.
The current interpolated local transform.
The current local transform.
The current world transform.
The position in world coordinates.
The rotation in world coordinates.
The scale in world coordinates.
Position in local coordinates.
Rotation in local coordinates.
Scale in local coordinates.
Called when the transform is changed

ConstructorArguments: Override to provide the local transform

Documentation.Summary: Override to provide the local transform

ConstructorArguments: Called when the local transform is being set
Override to provide the world transform. The default implementation will calculate it using GetLocalTransform() based on the parent.

Documentation.Summary: Override to provide the world transform. The default implementation will calculate it using GetLocalTransform() based on the parent.

ConstructorArguments: Called when the world transform is being set

Documentation.Summary: Called when the world transform is being set

ConstructorArguments: Tell our other components, and our children that our transform has changed. This will update things like Renderers to update their render positions.

Documentation.Summary:
Tell our other components, and our children that our transform has changed. This will
update things like Renderers to update their render positions.

ConstructorArguments: Help to implement a component that completely overrides the transform. This is useful for scenarios where you will want to keep the local transform of a GameObject, but want to offset based on that for some reason. Having multiple of these on one GameObject is not supported, and will result in weirdness.

Documentation.Summary:
Help to implement a component that completely overrides the transform. This is useful for scenarios
where you will want to keep the local transform of a GameObject, but want to offset based on that 
for some reason.
Having multiple of these on one GameObject is not supported, and will result in weirdness.

Describes a change to a <see cref="T:Sandbox.NetDictionary`2" /> which is passed to
<see cref="F:Sandbox.NetDictionary`2.OnChanged" /> whenever its contents change.
